---
title: "Eclipse PDT plugin on Kubuntu"
date: 2007-07-25
forum: Programming Talk
---

### Post by T1Oracle on 2007-07-25
I installed Eclipse on Kubuntu (originally installed as Ubuntu so I have GNOME too but I don't use it) using adept_installer from the Add/Remove Programs K menu item.  Then I tried to download <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/pdt/index.php">PDT</a> and <a href="http://europa-mirror1.eclipse.org/tools/pdt/downloads/?release=S20070611-M1">install it,</a> but there were no <a href="http://www.eclipse.org/pdt/install.php">install instructions</a> to be found on the site.  So, after hours of googling I found repeated mentions of copying the features and plugins folder to the Eclipse install folder.  Of course that was for other plugins, but since the zip for this plugin contained those two folders I decided to follow that advice (I guessed /usr/lib/eclipse as the install folder, it looked like it was the right one).

Now, Eclipse crashes when I open it and I get the following errors:
> *Eclipse pop up error message*
Error creating the view.
org.eclipse.ui.internal.ide.actions.CloseUnrelatedProjectsAction


> *Console output after following the Eclipse advice to run eclipse -clean when installing plugins*
bernard@bernard-desktop:~$ sudo eclipse -clean
searching for compatible vm...
  testing /usr/lib/jvm/java-gcj...found
X Error: BadDevice, invalid or uninitialized input device 166
  Major opcode:  146
  Minor opcode:  3
  Resource id:  0x0
Failed to open device
X Error: BadDevice, invalid or uninitialized input device 166
  Major opcode:  146
  Minor opcode:  3
  Resource id:  0x0
Failed to open device


The error log has the following output (this is long so I am putting it in a code tag)

Any help is appreciated.  I just want a decent IDE for PHP development.

---

### Post by oparnier on 2007-07-25
it seems that you have gcj installed on your system

Eclipse work better with the Sun's JVM
```
apt-get install sun-java6-jdk 
```

after try a 

```
java -version
```

you must now have :

```
java version "1.6.0"
Java(TM) SE Runtime Environment (build 1.6.0-b105)
Java HotSpot(TM) Client VM (build 1.6.0-b105, mixed mode, sharing)
```

now try to relaunch eclipse

---

### Post by T1Oracle on 2007-07-25
Thanks for the help, however my java version is still:
> java version "1.4.2"
gij (GNU libgcj) version 4.1.2 (Ubuntu 4.1.2-0ubuntu5)

Copyright (C) 2006 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
This is free software; see the source for copying conditions.  There is NO
warranty; not even for MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.


---

### Post by oparnier on 2007-07-26
Ok you have to tell to ubuntu to use the sun jdk by default

try
```
sudo update-alternatives --config java
sudo update-alternatives --config javac
sudo update-alternatives --config jar

```

and for each command choose the Sun jdk 6

then check again your java version, it should be correct!

### Post by T1Oracle on 2007-07-28

---

### Post by T1Oracle on 2007-07-29
NVM, Solved it.

I uninstalled it and followed the instructions here: [http://flurdy.com/docs/eclipse/install.html](http://flurdy.com/docs/eclipse/install.html)

Then I downloaded and extracted the PDT all in one package.  I don't know what made the difference but now it seems like I have two installations of Eclipse.  Maybe after I do another system backup I will experiment with removing the first one that doesn't have PDT.

Eitherway I have what I need to start PHP development so my mission is accomplished :)

---

### Post by petbse on 2007-08-12
My spec
Athlon XP 2200+ Thoroughbred
512MB DDR 400 RAM
Nvidia GeForce FX 5200
Feisty Fawn (ubuntu with kde installed - i use kde and sometimes gnome depends on mood)
kernel version: linux-generic-2.6.20.16.28.1
I followed instruction to get eclipse running and it runs fine. 
command java -version gives this:
> 
java version "1.6.0"
Java(TM) SE Runtime Environment (build 1.6.0-b105)
Java HotSpot(TM) Client VM (build 1.6.0-b105, mixed mode, sharing)


when i start eclipse from console it also finds java-6-sun as compatible jre
I had problems before with gcj so i setup suns java. My problem: I had to update to eclipse-3.3 Europa because of PDT plugin via eclipse update manager (i need to build cake-php site). PDT plugin needed WST version 2.0 that i found only in europa's discovery site and not on calisto's (i was not able to find any other eclipse discovery sites). I fear that all my troubles result from this upgrade. But I dont have any choice because PHPeclipse-1.18 runs only on eclipse 3.1 and I wasn't able to find any PDT version that would work on eclipse 3.2. 
The problem is i can't save files in pdt i can see them in my project(its located in my /home/petbse/projects) so its not an permissions issue i think. Eclipse looks like saving file it doesn't show any errors but when i look to file in nano or krusaders edit I dont see the changes. I am not sure if this is a java IO issue or Eclipse 3.3 not working in Feisty. Its weir but I was doing weird thing all day today :) I installed all needed plugins in sudo eclipse and via update manager. From following eclipse update sites:
[http://download.eclipse.org/tools/pdt/updates/](http://download.eclipse.org/tools/pdt/updates/)
[http://download.eclipse.org/releases/europa/](http://download.eclipse.org/releases/europa/)
and calisto but now after installs from europa is no longer in my list, so I dont remeber its url.
I was using phpeclipse for a while on windows but I am no more than firstime lucky noob with it :)
Any help is appreciated 
P.S. Hope i am not hijacking this thread but the problem scope is similar.

---

### Post by petbse on 2007-08-12
Bumping in 5 minutes I know I know](*,)](*,)
I created a new thread in case of this being hijack.
